title: US Coast Guard Rescues Four from Sinking Vessel off Florida Coast
summary: US Coast Guard launches rescue operation for distressed fishing vessel. Crew deploys helicopter and cutter to save four mariners. All survivors receive medical attention onshore.
contents:
## US Coast Guard Rescues Four from Sinking Vessel off Florida Coast

### Overview
US Coast Guard launches rescue operation for distressed fishing vessel. Crew deploys helicopter and cutter to save four mariners. All survivors receive medical attention onshore.

### Event Details
US Coast Guard Sector Miami launches urgent search-and-rescue mission after fishing vessel sends mayday call 25 miles east of Key Largo, Florida. Vessel takes on water rapidly due to hull breach during rough seas. Coast Guard Cutter William Trump responds immediately, deploys rescue swimmer via helicopter MH-65 Dolphin from Air Station Miami. Swimmer reaches sinking boat, assists four crew members into life raft. Helicopter hoists all four to safety, transports them to shore. Medical teams evaluate survivors for hypothermia and injuries; all report stable conditions. Incident highlights dangers of offshore fishing amid unpredictable weather. Coast Guard urges mariners to file float plans and carry EPIRBs. Detresfa_ posts real-time updates with video footage of hoist operation and cutter approach. Tweets detail vessel identification as 40-foot sportfisher 'Reel Deal,' coordinates 25°10'N 80°15'W. Agency coordinates with local authorities for vessel recovery efforts. Operation demonstrates effective inter-agency communication and rapid deployment capabilities. Fishermen credit VHF radio distress signal for swift response. Coast Guard continues monitoring area for additional hazards. Public shares Detresfa_ coverage widely, amplifying SAR awareness. Event underscores commitment to maritime safety in busy Florida Straits corridor.

### Source Context
@detresfa_ (Detresfa) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on detresfa maritime distress search rescue mayday sar coast guard vessel.
